steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.ille: 'ille' is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'that man (Catiline)'
hints.copias: 'copias' is a noun in the accusative meaning 'forces / troops'

hints.to educate: 'to educate' is 'educare' (1st conjugation) — 'educere' (3rd) means 'to lead out'

hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect indicative would be 'eduxit' — 'eduxisset' is pluperfect subjunctive
hints.Pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect indicative would be 'eduxerat' — 'eduxisset' with -isset ending is subjunctive
hints.Perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'eduxerit' — 'eduxisset' is pluperfect

steps.prompt: Why is the subjunctive used here?

hints.Purpose clause: Purpose clauses use 'ut/ne' — 'utinam' introduces a wish
hints.Result clause: Result clauses follow degree expressions — 'utinam' expresses a wish
hints.Indirect question: Indirect questions use interrogative words — 'utinam' introduces an unfulfilled wish

steps.target: An unfulfilled wish about the past — 'if only he had …'
choices: A present wish — 'if only he were …'
An unfulfilled wish about the past — 'if only he had …'

hints.A present wish — 'if only he were …': Present wish uses imperfect subjunctive — pluperfect subjunctive refers to the past
hints.A future hope — 'may he …': Future hopes use present subjunctive — pluperfect is past counterfactual
hints.A command — 'he must …': 'utinam' never introduces commands — it introduces wishes

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'ducere' (to lead) — the base verb without e- prefix
hints.educo, educare, educavi, educatum: These are the parts of 'educare' (to educate/raise) — a different 1st conjugation verb!
hints.seduco, seducere, seduxi, seductum: These are the parts of 'seducere' (to lead aside/separate)

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'copiae' — 'copias' with -as ending is accusative plural
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'copiarum' — 'copias' is accusative, object of 'eduxisset'

hints.2nd: 2nd declension accusative plural ends in -os — 'copia, copiae' is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension accusative plural ends in -es — 'copia, copiae' is 1st declension
hints.4th: 4th declension accusative plural ends in -us — 'copia, copiae' is 1st declension

translation: If only he had led out all his forces with him!
